event_name: Establishment of Tea Plantations in Ceylon
description: Large-scale tea cultivation introduced after the collapse of the coffee industry caused by coffee leaf rust
source_references: British Plantation Records; Sri Lanka Tea Board Archive; Colonial Agricultural Reports; Wikipedia

event_name: Expansion of the Ceylon Railway Network
description: Railway lines expanded to connect plantation regions with ports to facilitate export logistics
source_references: Ceylon Railway Department Records; British Colonial Blue Books; National Archives Sri Lanka

event_name: Decline of Coffee Plantations in Ceylon
description: Coffee plantations declined rapidly due to widespread coffee leaf rust disease
source_references: Colonial Agricultural Journals; Plantation Industry Reports; Wikipedia

event_name: Development of Colombo Port as a Colonial Trade Hub
description: Port infrastructure expanded to handle increasing volumes of tea and commodity exports
source_references: Sri Lanka Ports Authority Archive; British Trade Ledgers; Colonial Shipping Records; UNESCO Trade Routes

event_name: Introduction of Steam-Powered Machinery in Plantations
description: Steam-powered machinery adopted in tea processing to improve efficiency and production scale
source_references: Colonial Engineering Reports; Plantation Machinery Manuals

event_name: Expansion of British Plantation Administration
description: British colonial administration expanded oversight and regulation of plantation economies
source_references: British Colonial Blue Books; Administrative Correspondence; National Archives Sri Lanka

event_name: Migration of South Indian Tamil Labor to Plantations
description: Large-scale migration of laborers from South India to work on coffee and tea plantations
source_references: Colonial Census Records; Labor Migration Studies; Plantation Reports; Wikipedia

event_name: Establishment of Plantation-Based Export Economy
description: Shift from subsistence agriculture toward an export-oriented plantation economy
source_references: Colonial Economic Surveys; British Trade Statistics; Sri Lanka Economic History Texts

event_name: Introduction of Modern Irrigation for Plantations
description: Irrigation techniques introduced to stabilize plantation output and mitigate climate variability
source_references: Colonial Engineering Surveys; Agricultural Development Reports

event_name: Urban Growth of Colombo Under Colonial Rule
description: Colombo expanded rapidly as a commercial and administrative center under British colonial rule
source_references: Colonial Urban Planning Records; Census Data; British Administrative Reports; Wikipedia

event_name: Expansion of Export-Oriented Road Networks
description: Road networks expanded to connect inland plantations with coastal ports
source_references: Public Works Department Reports; Colonial Maps; National Archives Sri Lanka

event_name: Formalization of Tea Auction System in Colombo
description: Tea auction system established to regulate pricing and streamline global tea trade
source_references: Tea Trade Association Records; Colombo Commercial Archives; British Trade Publications

event_name: Introduction of Buddhism to Sri Lanka (Mahinda Mission)
description: Mission led by Mahinda (traditionally linked to Emperor Ashoka’s Mauryan court) introduced Buddhism, embedding Sri Lanka in trans-Asian Buddhist networks that later connected India, Southeast Asia, and the wider Indian Ocean.
exhibit_name: Ancient Sri Lanka & Indian Ocean Exchange Exhibit

event_name: Arrival of Prince Vijaya and Indo-Aryan Settlement Traditions
description: Foundational migration narratives link early state formation to broader South Asian population movements and cultural diffusion across the Bay of Bengal region.
exhibit_name: Origins of Sri Lankan Polity Exhibit

event_name: Early Indian Ocean Trade via Mantai (Mahathitha) Port
description: Maritime trade through Mantai integrated Sri Lanka into Indian Ocean exchange with South India, Arabia, and the Mediterranean, enabling flows of goods (gems, pearls) and ideas.

event_name: Roman-Era Trade Links and Coin Circulation in Sri Lanka
description: Roman coins and trade references indicate long-distance commerce, positioning Sri Lanka within early global trade routes connecting the Mediterranean to South Asia.

event_name: Construction of Major Ancient Irrigation Works (Tank Civilization Expansion)
location: North Central & Dry Zone, Sri Lanka
description: Large-scale hydraulic engineering (tanks, canals, sluices) reflects technology transfer and regional engineering parallels across South Asia, supporting surplus and political centralization.

event_name: Relic Diplomacy: Tooth Relic Traditions and Regional Buddhist Legitimacy
description: Sacred relic traditions strengthened ties with regional Buddhist polities and later influenced diplomatic-religious exchanges across South and Southeast Asia.

event_name: Chola Invasion and Integration into a South Indian Imperial System
description: Chola conquest linked Sri Lanka to South Indian imperial administration and Indian Ocean trade, reshaping governance, military logistics, and regional political economy.
exhibit_name: Medieval South Asia & Sri Lanka Exhibit

event_name: Rise of the Jaffna Kingdom and Indian Ocean Maritime Connections
description: Jaffna’s emergence strengthened maritime and mercantile ties with South India and wider Indian Ocean networks, shaping regional trade and cultural exchange.

event_name: Portuguese Arrival and Integration of Sri Lanka into Early Modern European Maritime Empire
location: Coastal Sri Lanka (Colombo and maritime regions)
description: Portuguese entry brought Sri Lanka into European imperial competition and global maritime trade, altering coastal governance, fortification systems, and religious dynamics.

event_name: Dutch Intervention and VOC Trade Monopoly Dynamics
description: Dutch–Kandyan alliance and VOC expansion tied Sri Lanka to Dutch global commerce and monopoly strategies, transforming taxation, cinnamon trade, and coastal administration.

event_name: Kandyan Convention and Cession of Sovereignty to the British Crown
description: Treaty-based transfer of sovereignty formalized British imperial control, linking Sri Lanka’s governance to global British strategic priorities in the Indian Ocean.

event_name: Colebrooke–Cameron Reforms and Modern Colonial Administration
description: Administrative reforms reorganized governance, courts, and fiscal systems, aligning Sri Lanka with British colonial institutional models used across the Empire.

event_name: Crown Lands Ordinance and Enclosure-Style Land Transformation
description: Land policies facilitated plantation expansion and mirrored broader imperial land regimes, enabling global commodity production through dispossession and reallocation.

event_name: Cinnamon Monopoly and Global Spice Commerce under European Rule
description: European control of cinnamon production tied Sri Lanka to global spice markets and monopoly trade systems, influencing international pricing and merchant networks.

event_name: Uva Rebellion and British Counterinsurgency (Scorched Earth Policy)
description: Armed resistance and harsh suppression illustrate imperial security practices and administrative consolidation, comparable to other British colonial counterinsurgencies.

event_name: Opening of the Suez Canal and Acceleration of Tea/Commodity Shipping Routes
location: Indian Ocean route affecting Sri Lanka–Europe trade
description: Suez Canal reshaped global maritime logistics, shortening routes to Europe and strengthening Sri Lanka’s plantation exports in British markets.

event_name: Global Coffee Rust Crisis and Shift to Tea as an Export Commodity
description: Coffee leaf rust contributed to a global commodity shock, pushing Sri Lanka and other colonies toward tea, reshaping international plantation agriculture and markets.

event_name: Expansion of Indian Indentured/Recruitment Systems for Plantation Labor
location: From South India to Sri Lanka (Hill Country estates)
description: Cross-border labor recruitment connected Sri Lanka’s plantations to wider imperial labor regimes and demographic flows across the British Empire.

event_name: Institutionalization of Colombo as an Indian Ocean Naval and Coaling Hub
description: Strategic port development supported imperial sea-lane control and connected Sri Lanka to global naval logistics and maritime security networks.

event_name: Trincomalee Harbour as a British Strategic Base in the Indian Ocean
description: Trincomalee’s naval value linked Sri Lanka to global British defense planning, sea-lane control, and wartime logistics across the Indian Ocean theatre.

event_name: Donoughmore Commission and Introduction of Universal Franchise
description: Constitutional reforms introduced broad franchise early in Asia, shaped by imperial governance debates and influencing political development in the late-colonial world.

event_name: Soulbury Commission and Pathway to Dominion Status
description: Late-colonial constitutional negotiations reflected global decolonization dynamics and British policy shifts after World War II.

event_name: World War II Indian Ocean Raid and Colombo Bombing (Strategic Shock)
description: Japanese naval attacks made Sri Lanka a front-line base, integrating local security and logistics into global wartime operations in the Indian Ocean.
exhibit_name: World War II & Sri Lanka Exhibit

event_name: Tea Research and Agronomic Experimentation (Assam Linkages and Knowledge Transfer)
description: Research missions and agronomic exchange with Assam and wider imperial networks supported plantation modernization and global tea industry standards.

event_name: Expansion of Missionary Education and English-Language Institutions under Colonial Rule
location: Colombo and provincial centers, Sri Lanka
description: Missionary schools and English education linked Sri Lanka to global religious and educational networks, shaping an English-educated elite and administrative workforce.

event_name: Rise of Plantation-Linked Global Commodity Finance and Agency Houses
description: Agency houses and finance structures connected Sri Lankan plantations to global capital markets, insurance, and commodity pricing mechanisms.

event_name: Great Depression Impacts on Tea and Rubber Prices (Global Demand Shock)
description: Global price collapses affected plantation incomes, labor relations, and colonial fiscal policy, demonstrating dependence on international commodity cycles.

event_name: Development of Rubber Plantations in Response to Global Industrial Demand
description: Rubber expansion aligned with global demand for industrial and automotive supply chains, linking Sri Lanka to international manufacturing inputs.

event_name: Coconut Copra and Desiccated Coconut Exports in Global Food and Industrial Markets
description: Coconut-based exports integrated Sri Lanka into global food, soap, and industrial supply chains, shaping rural cash-crop economies.

event_name: Formation of Ceylon National Congress and Early Anti-Colonial Politics
description: Political mobilization reflected global currents of self-determination after World War I and connected Sri Lanka’s elites to wider anti-colonial and constitutional reform movements.

event_name: Establishment of the University of Ceylon
description: Founded as the first modern university in Ceylon; major institution-building in late colonial period.

event_name: Arrival of the Sacred Bodhi Tree
description: The sacred Jaya Sri Maha Bodhi tree was brought to the city.

event_name: Royal College, Colombo
description: Royal College, Colombo founded

event_name: Introduction of Cricket to Sri Lanka
description: Cricket introduced to Sri Lanka

event_name: San Antonio College Established
description: San Antonio College was established in 1642 in Colombo by Franciscan missionaries and is regarded as one of the earliest formal schools in Sri Lanka.
